evidence.summary: Agent skill installer — copies the 'launchdeck-agent' skill into AI coding agent CLI toolchains (Codex CLI, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Visual Studio) at project or user scope.

exports.name: listAgentTargets
exports.description: Returns all 8 target descriptors (4 adapters x 2 scopes) plus validated canonical source

exports.name: doctorAgentInstaller
exports.signature: (options = {}) => { ok, status, summary, source, targets, checks }
exports.description: Health check: validates source existence, manifest validity, and adapter matrix completeness

exports.name: installAgentSkill
exports.signature: (options = {}) => { agent, scope, dryRun, force, source, target, result }
exports.description: Main install function. Validates source manifest, resolves target, compares directories, plans actions, applies if not dryRun.

exports.name: supportedAgents
exports.description: Returns ['codex', 'claude-code', 'github-copilot', 'visual-studio']

internal_functions: requireAgent(agentId) — validates agent ID against ADAPTERS
normalizeScope(scope) — validates 'project' or 'user'
validateCanonicalSource(options) — checks source dir + SKILL.md manifest declares name: launchdeck-agent
targetDescriptor(adapter, scope, projectRoot, homeDir) — builds target path descriptor
explicitTargetDescriptor(adapter, scope, target, projectRoot) — builds custom target path
compareSkillDirectories(sourceDir, targetDir) — binary-content diff between source and target dirs
listFiles(root) / collectFiles(root, relativeDir, files) — recursive file listing, rejects symlinks
sameFileContent(left, right) — Buffer.equals comparison
planInstallActions(sourceDir, target, comparison, force) — mkdir, copy_file, update_file
applyInstallActions(actions) — executes planned filesystem actions
installStatus(comparison, actions, dryRun) — 'already_installed' | 'planned' | 'installed'
key_design_points: Adapter matrix: 4 agents x 2 scopes = 8 install targets
Source is always .agents/skills/launchdeck-agent/ in the package root
Binary content comparison using Buffer.equals (not mtime/size)
Refuses to install if source SKILL.md is missing or invalid
Refuses to install if target diverges from source without --force
Dry-run mode returns planned actions without applying them
Symbolic links in agent skill packages are rejected with error

evidence.summary: Cross-platform path safety adapter — path canonicalization, containment assertions, and clean-target validation with platform-aware case sensitivity.

exports.name: canonicalPath
exports.description: Resolves relative to basePath, normalizes, optionally checks existence and resolves symlinks

exports.name: canonicalProjectRoot
exports.description: Convenience wrapper around canonicalPath with project-root defaults

exports.name: resolveProjectPath
exports.description: Resolves a path and asserts it stays inside project root

exports.name: pathContains
exports.description: Returns true if target is under parent (allowRoot controls root-match behavior)

exports.name: assertContainedPath
exports.description: Throws LaunchdeckError with code 'project_root_escape' if path escapes project root

exports.name: isSamePath
exports.description: Compares two paths for equality (case-insensitive on win32)

exports.name: assertProjectRelativePath
exports.description: Throws if path is absolute (checks win32 and posix isAbsolute)

exports.name: resolveCleanTarget
exports.description: Validates relative, contained, non-root path for clean operations

exports.name: planCleanTarget
exports.description: Full planning pipeline: validates, resolves, checks existence, symlinks, containment, returns status 'planned'|'refused'|'skipped_missing'

exports.name: assertSafeCleanTarget
exports.description: Variant that also refuses project root itself as a clean target

exports.name: toProjectRelativePath
exports.description: Converts absolute path to project-relative

exports.name: pathForPlatform
exports.description: Returns the appropriate path API module for the platform

internal_functions: normalizePathValue(value, label, code)
comparablePath(value, platform) — normalizes and lowercases for case-insensitive platforms
safeCanonicalProjectRoot(projectRoot, platform) — returns undefined on failure
refusedCleanTarget(record, refusalCode, extra)
isSameComparablePath(left, right, platform)
pathContainsComparable(parentPath, targetPath, options)
key_design_points: Case-insensitive path comparison for win32 using comparablePath (normalize + toLowerCase)
refusalCode system for planCleanTarget: 'clean_target_empty', 'clean_target_ambiguous', 'clean_target_root', 'clean_target_outside_project'
symlink resolution via fs.realpathSync.native for canonical containment checks
Dual isAbsolute check (win32 + posix) ensures correct behavior on all platforms
planCleanTarget handles both string and object targetPath inputs
safeCanonicalProjectRoot guards against project root resolution failures

evidence.summary: Cross-platform process lifecycle adapter — spawn, manage, inspect, and terminate processes with platform-specific implementations.

exports.name: spawnForeground
exports.description: Spawns process with inherit stdio, returns exit promise

exports.name: spawnManaged
exports.description: Spawns detached process, unrefs it, returns control record

exports.name: spawnShellCommand
exports.description: Low-level spawn with smart shell detection (bypasses shell for known node executables)

exports.name: waitForExit
exports.description: Promise wrapper for child process exit

exports.name: isPidRunning
exports.description: Uses process.kill(pid, 0) — EPERM means alive, ESRCH means dead

exports.name: getLiveness
exports.description: Wraps isPidRunning with try-catch

exports.name: getProcessEvidence
exports.description: Returns detailed process info: Windows uses CIM/WMI, POSIX uses ps + procfs

exports.name: listPortListeners
exports.description: Lists TCP/UDP port listeners: Windows uses netstat -ano, POSIX tries lsof -> ss -> netstat fallback chain

exports.name: stopProcessTree
exports.signature: (pid, options = {}) => Promise<{ pid, ok, status, method }>
exports.description: Async process tree termination: Windows uses taskkill /T, POSIX sends signal to negative PID (process group)

exports.name: stopProcessTreeSync
exports.description: Synchronous version of stopProcessTree

exports.name: normalizePid
exports.description: Validates PID is a positive integer; with allowMissing returns undefined for empty/null

exports.name: normalizePort
exports.description: Validates port is an integer 1-65535

exports.name: classifyAdapterError
exports.description: Wraps non-LaunchdeckError into LaunchdeckError with code 'adapter_failed'

internal_functions: normalizeCommand(command) — validates non-empty string
planSpawn(command, shell) — optimizes out shell wrapper for known node executables
parseSimpleCommand(command) — tokenizes command, returns null if shell metacharacters present
isNodeExecutable(command) — compares against 'node', 'node.exe', process.execPath
listWindowsPortListeners(port) — parses netstat -ano output
windowsProcessEvidence(pid, options) — Get-CimInstance Win32_Process via PowerShell
posixProcessEvidence(pid, options) — ps -o lstart,command, plus /proc/pid/cwd
posixProcessCwd(pid) — reads /proc/pid/cwd symlink
parseWindowsNetstatLine(line) — parses one netstat line (TCP state=LISTENING or UDP)
listPosixPortListeners(port) — fallback chain: lsof -> ss -> netstat
parseLsofLine / parseSsLine / parsePosixNetstatLine
parseEndpoint(endpoint) — parses 'addr:port' including IPv6 [addr]:port
dedupeListeners(listeners) — deduplicates by protocol+address+port+pid+state+source
taskkillProcessTree(pid, options) — /T /F, with ETIMEDOUT fallback to process.kill
signalPosixProcessTree(pid, signal) — negative-PID kill with single-PID fallback
waitForNotRunning / waitForNotRunningSync — polling loops with 50ms interval
classifyStopFailure / signalToExitCode / delay / sleepSync
key_design_points: Shell optimization: detects node executables and spawns directly without shell wrapping, reducing process overhead
Shell metacharacter detection: if command contains |&;<>()`, always uses shell
Three-tier port listener fallback on POSIX (lsof -> ss -> netstat) for maximum compatibility
Process tree termination: Windows taskkill /T /F with timeout fallback; POSIX kill(-pid) with single-pid fallback
Process evidence via platform-native tools: Windows CIM, POSIX ps + procfs
All cross-platform branching is explicit: if (platform === 'win32') patterns
Comprehensive error classification — all errors wrapped into LaunchdeckError with code 'adapter_failed' or 'stop_failed'
sleepSync uses Atomics.wait on SharedArrayBuffer (blocking, no setTimeout)

evidence.summary: CLI lifecycle smoke test — runs the official demo through the complete Launchdeck CLI lifecycle in an isolated temporary workspace.
symbols: REPO_ROOT, CLI_PATH, OFFICIAL_DEMO_PATH, DEMO_ALIAS, DEMO_TASK, DEMO_TARGET, DEMO_PORT (consts)
internal_functions: parseArgs(argv) — --mode quick|full, --quickstart, --keep, --json, --help
createWorkspace() — mkdtemp, cp demo with .launchdeck/scratch filter, create clean-safe artifact
buildEnv(home) — sets LAUNCHDECK_HOME + NO_COLOR
runCli(step, args, context, options) — spawns CLI, captures stdout/stderr, pushes result to context.steps
parseJson(stepResult) — JSON.parse with error wrapper
assertExit(result, expectedCode) / assertJsonOk(result) / checkedStep(context, step, args)
assertExistingRun(result) — verifies spawned=false in duplicate start
stopDemoIfOwned(context) — attempts stop in finally block
runSmoke(options) — full lifecycle pipeline with mode branching
printHuman(evidence) / main()
key_design_points: Mode quick: project add, start, duplicate start, status, inspect (task+port), restart, stop, reconcile, clean --safe
Mode full adds: projects, ps, ports, conflicts, logs, events, project remove
Temporary workspace + isolated LAUNCHDECK_HOME — never mutates source demo
Finally block always attempts cleanup stop + rm -rf temp workspace (unless --keep)
--json mode outputs structured evidence object; --quickstart maps to --mode full
Safety guarantees documented in evidence output: local evidence only, cross-platform claim=false, no privileged hooks, no arbitrary process kill
Step results tracked as array of { step, command, cwd, exitCode, signal, stdout, stderr, expectedFailure }

evidence.summary: CI workflow — 3 OS matrix (windows-latest, macos-latest, ubuntu-latest) running check, test, and inline lifecycle smoke.
key_design_points: Matrix: windows-latest, macos-latest, ubuntu-latest with fail-fast: false
Node.js 20 with npm cache via setup-node@v4
Steps: Checkout -> Setup Node -> npm ci -> npm run check -> npm test -> lifecycle smoke
Lifecycle smoke is a large inline Node script (not a script reference), creating a CI smoke project from scratch

Inline smoke covers: doctor, tasks (build/dev/fail), run build, run fail (expects exit 7), start dev, duplicate start (expects conflict), ps, logs, stop, second stop, clean dry-run, clean --safe, clean --all (expects confirmation needed)
No artifact uploads — smoke test is purely exit-code-gated

evidence.summary: Shared error system — defines all Launchdeck error codes, aliases, and the LaunchdeckError class with payload serialization.

exports.name: ERROR_CODES
exports.description: Canonical set of ~50 known error codes

exports.name: ERROR_CODE_ALIASES
exports.description: Maps legacy/variant codes to canonical codes (e.g. 'invalid_arguments' -> 'command_usage_error')

exports.name: LaunchdeckError
exports.description: extends Error with .code and .details properties

exports.name: toContractErrorCode
exports.description: Maps any code to canonical via ERROR_CODE_ALIASES, defaulting to 'internal_error'

exports.name: toContractErrorPayload
exports.description: toErrorPayload with contractCode: true

key_design_points: Error codes defined as a Set for O(1) lookup
Alias map allows graceful migration of error codes
toContractErrorPayload provides a stable public API surface
LaunchdeckError stores arbitrary .details object and .next (action suggestions) array

summary.family_theme: Core services abstraction layer — platform-aware adapters for path safety, process lifecycle, and agent toolchain installation, backed by a shared error system. Validated by a comprehensive smoke lifecycle script and a 3-OS CI matrix.
